creator: listjm@club-internet.fr
text:
> Related to nspluginwrapper specifically: can you try to reinstall it
> now? There was a brief period of time when it was installing the
> plugin to the wrong place.

Sure! I deinstalled both nspluginwrapper and linux-f10-flasplugin11, 
reinstalled again and ran:
% nspluginwrapper -v -a -i

It installed the plugin and Firefox recognized it. Problem solved as 
long as the plugin is reinstalled. On the other hand, if I understand it 
well, just updating those two ports with e.g. portmaster will break the 
functionality in Firefox. Thatâs what I did at first, the typical weekly 
updating of ports.

Thanks again,
Juan
